summary:
Claim: Restricting dietary sugar reduced mortality in individuals exposed to poor hygiene.  
Evidence: - The Vipeholm studies (1937–1956) showed that sugar-restricted diets led to significantly lower death rates among children in unsanitary conditions. - Mortality benefits were especially evident as microbial exposure (proxy for poor hygiene) increased.  
Answer: C
formatted_summary:

Evidence: - The Vipeholm studies (1937–1956) showed that sugar-restricted diets led to significantly lower death rates among children in unsanitary conditions. - Mortality benefits were especially evident as microbial exposure (proxy for poor hygiene) increased.  
Answer: C. Answer choice: C
